name: ruby-nokogiri
title: ruby-nokogiri
summary: HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser for Ruby
description:
HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser for Ruby
Nokogiri (鋸) is an HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser.  It is able to
search documents via XPath or CSS3 selectors, and is a drop-in replacement
for Hpricot (though not bug for bug).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use nokogiri because HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser for Ruby.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use nokogiri as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `nokogiri $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for nokogiri.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `nokogiri ./public/index.html` as documented upstream for nokogiri.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl -s $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | nokogiri -e'p $_.css("h1").length'` as documented upstream for nokogiri.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair nokogiri with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `nokogiri --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install ruby-nokogiri. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `nokogiri $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `nokogiri ./public/index.html`; `curl -s $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | nokogiri -e'p $_.css("h1").length'`. HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser for Ruby.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser for Ruby.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: nokogiri - an HTML, XML, SAX, and Reader parser.
